findings.message: Sensitive data stored in browser storage — visible in DevTools → Application tab
findings.fix: Don't store tokens or sensitive data in localStorage/sessionStorage. Use httpOnly cookies (set by the server) for auth tokens — they're invisible to JavaScript and DevTools Application tab. For session data, keep it server-side.

findings.message: Server action "uploadImage" has no authentication check. Anyone can call it.
findings.fix: Add auth at the top of every exported server action (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) throw new Error('Unauthorized');") or wrap it with your auth guard. If it is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line nextjs-server-action-exposure".

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Direct innerHTML assignment with dynamic, unescaped value — potential XSS vector
findings.fix: Escape/sanitize the value before assigning to innerHTML (e.g. an esc()/DOMPurify wrapper), or use textContent for plain text.

findings.message: Direct innerHTML assignment with dynamic, unescaped value — potential XSS vector
findings.evidence: countEl.innerHTML = 'SHOWING ' + filtered.length + ' OF ' + BUSINESSES.length +
findings.fix: Escape/sanitize the value before assigning to innerHTML (e.g. an esc()/DOMPurify wrapper), or use textContent for plain text.

findings.message: Error logged with console.log instead of console.error
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Check-then-update on balance/inventory without lock — concurrent requests can cause inconsistency.
findings.fix: Use database transactions or atomic operations. For Prisma: prisma.$transaction(). For MongoDB: $inc for counters. For SQL: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE within a transaction.

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML used without sanitization — XSS vulnerability.
findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML used without sanitization — XSS vulnerability.
findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.message: Dockerfile has no USER directive — container runs as root by default.
findings.fix: Add a non-root user before CMD/ENTRYPOINT: "RUN addgroup -S app && adduser -S app -G app" then "USER app". One exploit as root = full system access.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy

findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Next.js mutation handler — verify CSRF protection is in place
findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: Mongoose find() with no limit() — returns all matching documents
findings.evidence: const match = stderr.split(/\r?\n/).find((l) => l.includes("PO Token Providers:"));
findings.fix: Always add pagination or limits to list endpoints. Use limit/offset, cursor-based pagination, or at minimum a hard cap (e.g., .limit(100)). Without limits: (1) anyone can scrape your entire database in one request, (2) large result sets crash your server or client, (3) it's a trivial DoS vector.

findings.evidence: console.log(`[social-publish] Access token for ${platform} is expiring. Refreshing...`);
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.message: Query builder select without limit — may return unbounded results
findings.fix: Always add pagination or limits to list endpoints. Use limit/offset, cursor-based pagination, or at minimum a hard cap (e.g., .limit(100)). Without limits: (1) anyone can scrape your entire database in one request, (2) large result sets crash your server or client, (3) it's a trivial DoS vector.

findings.message: Database query without field selection — consider selecting only needed fields
findings.fix: Only return the fields the client actually needs. Use select/projection in your database query, or map the result to a DTO (data transfer object) before sending. Anyone can see the full response in DevTools → Network → click request → Response tab.

findings.message: Button disabled based on client-side permission check — button can be re-enabled in DevTools Elements
findings.fix: Never trust client-side values for pricing, permissions, or business logic. Always recalculate/revalidate on the server. The client is the user's machine — they control everything on it. DevTools lets anyone modify any value, re-enable any button, and edit any request before it's sent.

findings.message: Middleware file contains no authentication or redirect logic.
findings.fix: Add auth checking in middleware: verify session/token and redirect unauthenticated users to login.

findings.message: API key hardcoded in client-side code — visible in DevTools Sources
findings.fix: Never put secrets in client-side code. Move sensitive operations to a server-side API route. The browser bundle is fully readable — open DevTools → Sources and search for any string.

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.evidence: // Force Node runtime so the cookie clear from `signOut()` actually
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: Secret passed as URL query parameter — logged in server logs, browser history, and Referer headers
findings.fix: Never pass secrets in URL query parameters. Use Authorization headers instead: "headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + token }". URLs are logged in server access logs, proxy logs, CDN logs, browser history, and sent in the Referer header to every external site you link to.

findings.message: Exported POST handler has no authentication check.
findings.fix: Add an auth check at the top of the handler (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) return Response.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 })") or wrap it with your auth guard. If this route is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line missing-auth".

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML used without sanitization — XSS vulnerability.
findings.evidence: // surface. We avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's normal
findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Non-localhost HTTP URL — data sent unencrypted
findings.fix: Use HTTPS for all external connections. HTTP transmits data in plaintext — anyone on the network can read it.

findings.message: Error details returned in API response
findings.evidence: return NextResponse.json({ error: "Validation failed", details: parsed.error.format() }, { status: 400 });
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Next.js mutation handler — verify CSRF protection is in place
findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: Supabase query without limit/range — returns all matching rows
findings.fix: Always add pagination or limits to list endpoints. Use limit/offset, cursor-based pagination, or at minimum a hard cap (e.g., .limit(100)). Without limits: (1) anyone can scrape your entire database in one request, (2) large result sets crash your server or client, (3) it's a trivial DoS vector.

findings.fix: Remove PII from log statements or redact sensitive fields before logging. Use structured logging with field allowlists.

findings.message: Headers are configured but X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors is missing — vulnerable to clickjacking.
findings.fix: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY header, or use CSP frame-ancestors: 'none'. If using helmet: helmet() sets this by default.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: vercel.json has no security headers configured.
findings.fix: Add a "headers" section with X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Content-Security-Policy.

findings.message: Hardcoded IP address 76.76.21.21 — should be in an environment variable.
findings.evidence: Maps a name directly to a server's numeric IP address. E.g., routing `digitalalchemy.dev` to Vercel's IP address `76.
findings.fix: Move the IP address to an environment variable: process.env.SERVICE_HOST. Hardcoded IPs make deployment inflexible and may expose infrastructure details.

findings.message: Error logged with console.log instead of console.error
findings.evidence: console.log('[Service Worker] Fetch failed, serving offline page:', error);
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Query builder select without limit — may return unbounded results
findings.fix: Always add pagination or limits to list endpoints. Use limit/offset, cursor-based pagination, or at minimum a hard cap (e.g., .limit(100)). Without limits: (1) anyone can scrape your entire database in one request, (2) large result sets crash your server or client, (3) it's a trivial DoS vector.

findings.message: Query builder select without limit — may return unbounded results
findings.evidence: let query = db.from("subscribers").select("id, email, unsubscribe_token").eq("confirmed", true);
findings.fix: Always add pagination or limits to list endpoints. Use limit/offset, cursor-based pagination, or at minimum a hard cap (e.g., .limit(100)). Without limits: (1) anyone can scrape your entire database in one request, (2) large result sets crash your server or client, (3) it's a trivial DoS vector.

findings.message: Database query without field selection — consider selecting only needed fields
findings.fix: Only return the fields the client actually needs. Use select/projection in your database query, or map the result to a DTO (data transfer object) before sending. Anyone can see the full response in DevTools → Network → click request → Response tab.

findings.message: Secret passed as URL query parameter — logged in server logs, browser history, and Referer headers
findings.fix: Never pass secrets in URL query parameters. Use Authorization headers instead: "headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + token }". URLs are logged in server access logs, proxy logs, CDN logs, browser history, and sent in the Referer header to every external site you link to.

findings.message: "VITE_DATABASE_URL" exposes a secret to the browser. The VITE_ prefix makes this variable public in your build output.
findings.fix: Remove the "VITE_" prefix from this variable. Server-side secrets must NOT use client-prefixed env vars. Access it only in server-side code (API routes, server components, middleware).

findings.message: CORS allows all origins — any website can call your API
findings.fix: Configure CORS with specific allowed origins instead of "*". List only the domains that need access to your API.

findings.message: Google API key found in source code.
findings.fix: Move this Google API key to an environment variable. Add the variable to .env (git-ignored) and reference it via process.env.YOUR_KEY_NAME. Never commit secrets to source control.

findings.fix: Remove the "VITE_" prefix from this variable. Server-side secrets must NOT use client-prefixed env vars. Access it only in server-side code (API routes, server components, middleware).

findings.message: Storage rules allow unrestricted access. Anyone on the internet can read/write your data.
findings.evidence: "evidence": "prompt: `SECURITY FIX: My Firebase/Firestore security rules allow public read/write access. Replace \"allow read, write: if true\" with proper rules. At minimum: \"allow read, write: if request.auth != null\" (requires login). Better: add per-collection rules that check request.auth.uid matches the document's userId field. Test rules in the Firebase Emulator before deploying. Never use \"if true\" in production.`,",
findings.fix: Require authentication: "allow read, write: if request.auth != null;" at minimum. Better: add granular per-collection rules that validate request.auth.uid matches the document owner.

findings.message: Rule allows unrestricted access (if true). Anyone on the internet can read/write your data.
findings.fix: Require authentication: "allow read, write: if request.auth != null;" at minimum. Better: add granular per-collection rules that validate request.auth.uid matches the document owner.

findings.message: eval() with dynamic input — code injection risk
findings.fix: Sanitize all user input before use. For HTML: use a sanitization library or textContent instead of innerHTML. For SQL: use parameterized queries. For shell: use allowlists, never interpolate user input into commands.

findings.message: SQL query built with string interpolation — SQL injection risk
findings.evidence: "evidence": "const query = db.execute(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '${userInput}'`); // SQL injection",
findings.fix: Sanitize all user input before use. For HTML: use a sanitization library or textContent instead of innerHTML. For SQL: use parameterized queries. For shell: use allowlists, never interpolate user input into commands.

findings.fix: Never expose the service_role key to the client. Use the anon key for browser Supabase calls, and keep service_role usage in server-only code (route handlers, server actions, or files importing "server-only").

findings.message: Server action "hasDirective" has no authentication check. Anyone can call it.
findings.fix: Add auth at the top of every exported server action (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) throw new Error('Unauthorized');") or wrap it with your auth guard. If it is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line nextjs-server-action-exposure".

findings.fix: Verify the webhook: const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET). Never parse the body as JSON first — use the raw body.

findings.message: Storage rules allow unrestricted access. Anyone on the internet can read/write your data.
findings.evidence: prompt: `SECURITY FIX: My Firebase/Firestore security rules allow public read/write access. Replace "allow read, write: if true" with proper rules. At minimum: "allow read, write: if request.auth != null" (requires login). Better: add per-collection rules that check request.auth.uid matches the document's userId field. Test rules in the Firebase Emulator before deploying. Never use "if true" in production.`,
findings.fix: Require authentication: "allow read, write: if request.auth != null;" at minimum. Better: add granular per-collection rules that validate request.auth.uid matches the document owner.

findings.evidence: prompt: `SECURITY FIX: I have insecure connection configurations. Find and fix: (1) HTTP URLs pointing to production ser
findings.fix: Never disable TLS verification. Fix the underlying certificate issue instead. In production this allows man-in-the-middle attacks.

findings.message: Function "GET" uses a user-supplied ID without verifying ownership in the same function scope.
findings.fix: After extracting the ID, verify ownership within the same function: "if (resource.userId !== session.user.id) return 403".

findings.message: Function "uploadHandler" handles file uploads without type/size validation in scope.
findings.fix: Validate MIME type against an allowlist and set a max file size. The HTML accept="" attribute is NOT security.

findings.message: Function "POST" passes raw request body to a database operation without destructuring or schema validation first.
findings.evidence: const user = await prisma.user.create({ data: body });
findings.fix: Destructure only expected fields: "const { name, email } = req.body" then pass those explicitly. Or validate with Zod: "schema.parse(req.body)".

findings.message: Find-then-create race condition (possible duplicate) — concurrent requests can cause inconsistency.
findings.fix: Use database transactions or atomic operations. For Prisma: prisma.$transaction(). For MongoDB: $inc for counters. For SQL: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE within a transaction.

findings.message: Webhook handler does not verify the request signature. Anyone who knows the URL can send fake events (e.g., fake "payment succeeded").
findings.fix: Always verify webhook signatures before processing events. For Stripe: use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(body, sig, secret). For other providers: verify the HMAC signature using your webhook secret. Without verification, attackers can POST fake events to trigger actions like granting access, issuing refunds, or creating accounts.

findings.message: Stripe webhook handler does not verify the signature — anyone can send fake events.

findings.message: Sensitive data stored in browser storage — visible in DevTools → Application tab
findings.fix: Don't store tokens or sensitive data in localStorage/sessionStorage. Use httpOnly cookies (set by the server) for auth tokens — they're invisible to JavaScript and DevTools Application tab. For session data, keep it server-side.

findings.message: Direct innerHTML assignment with dynamic, unescaped value — potential XSS vector
findings.fix: Escape/sanitize the value before assigning to innerHTML (e.g. an esc()/DOMPurify wrapper), or use textContent for plain text.

findings.message: eval() with dynamic input — code injection risk
findings.fix: Sanitize all user input before use. For HTML: use a sanitization library or textContent instead of innerHTML. For SQL: use parameterized queries. For shell: use allowlists, never interpolate user input into commands.

findings.message: SQL query built with string interpolation — SQL injection risk

findings.fix: Sanitize all user input before use. For HTML: use a sanitization library or textContent instead of innerHTML. For SQL: use parameterized queries. For shell: use allowlists, never interpolate user input into commands.

findings.message: CORS allows all origins — any website can call your API
findings.fix: Configure CORS with specific allowed origins instead of "*". List only the domains that need access to your API.

findings.message: Non-localhost HTTP URL — data sent unencrypted
findings.fix: Use HTTPS for all external connections. HTTP transmits data in plaintext — anyone on the network can read it.

findings.message: Sensitive data in console.log — visible in DevTools Console
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.message: Server redirect using unvalidated user input — open redirect vulnerability
findings.fix: Validate redirect URLs before use: (1) Only allow relative paths (startsWith("/")). (2) If absolute URLs are needed, check against an allowlist of your own domains. (3) Never redirect to user-provided external URLs. Without validation, /login?redirect=https://evil.com sends authenticated users to the attacker's phishing site.

findings.fix: Use environment variables for debug flags: "debug: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'". Never hardcode debug:true. Disable GraphQL introspection/playground in production. Debug mode exposes stack traces, internal routes, database queries, and environment variables to anyone.

findings.message: MFA verification endpoint may not be linked to the login session — could be called independently.
findings.fix: Link MFA verification to the login session. Issue a temporary "pending MFA" token after password auth, require it for the MFA verify endpoint, and only issue a full session after both steps pass.

findings.message: JWT issued without expiration — token is valid forever if compromised.
findings.evidence: prompt: `SECURITY FIX: My JWT implementation has security issues. Check my JWT code for: (1) Weak signing secret — repla
findings.fix: Add expiry to jwt.sign: jwt.sign(payload, secret, { expiresIn: "1h" }). Use short-lived access tokens (15min-1h) with refresh tokens for longer sessions.

findings.message: API route calls a paid service but has no rate limiting. A bot or abusive user could run up your bill.
findings.fix: Add rate limiting before calling paid APIs. Use Upstash Ratelimit (serverless-friendly) or express-rate-limit. Set sensible per-user and global limits. Also consider adding spend alerts on your API provider dashboard.

findings.message: Next.js mutation handler — verify CSRF protection is in place
findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: AI API call has no max_tokens limit — uncapped cost per request.
findings.fix: Add max_tokens (e.g., max_tokens: 1000) to limit cost per request. Also add rate limiting to the API route and set spend alerts on your AI provider dashboard.

findings.message: AI API call has no max_tokens limit — uncapped cost per request.

findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Production source maps enabled in Next.js — full source code exposed
findings.fix: Disable source maps for production builds, or use "hidden-source-map" which generates maps but doesn't reference them in the bundle. Source maps let anyone see your full original code, comments, and variable names in DevTools → Sources.

findings.message: Auth check in useEffect — runs client-side only, server data is still exposed
findings.fix: Client-side auth only hides UI elements — it does NOT protect data. Always verify auth server-side in your API routes/middleware too. Anyone can open DevTools Console and call fetch("/your/api/endpoint") directly.

findings.message: Admin content hidden via conditional render — visible by calling API directly in DevTools
findings.fix: Client-side auth only hides UI elements — it does NOT protect data. Always verify auth server-side in your API routes/middleware too. Anyone can open DevTools Console and call fetch("/your/api/endpoint") directly.

findings.message: Price/total calculated client-side with arithmetic — can be modified via DevTools Console before submission
findings.evidence: const totalPrice = cart.reduce((sum, item) => sum + item.price * item.quantity, 0);
findings.fix: Never trust client-side values for pricing, permissions, or business logic. Always recalculate/revalidate on the server. The client is the user's machine — they control everything on it. DevTools lets anyone modify any value, re-enable any button, and edit any request before it's sent.

findings.message: Stack trace potentially exposed
findings.evidence: res.json({ error: err.message, stack: err.stack }); // Error leak
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Variable "API_KEY" contains a high-entropy string (entropy: 5.2) — possible hardcoded secret.
findings.fix: Move this value to an environment variable. If it's a secret, add it to .env (git-ignored) and reference via process.env.

findings.message: Redirect using unvalidated user input — open redirect risk
findings.fix: Sanitize all user input before use. For HTML: use a sanitization library or textContent instead of innerHTML. For SQL: use parameterized queries. For shell: use allowlists, never interpolate user input into commands.

findings.message: Empty catch block — errors silently swallowed
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: SQL query built with string interpolation — SQL injection risk
findings.evidence: await sql.query(`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "${t}" CASCADE`);
findings.fix: Sanitize all user input before use. For HTML: use a sanitization library or textContent instead of innerHTML. For SQL: use parameterized queries. For shell: use allowlists, never interpolate user input into commands.

findings.message: Exported GET handler has no authentication check.
findings.evidence: export async function GET(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
findings.fix: Add an auth check at the top of the handler (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) return Response.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 })") or wrap it with your auth guard. If this route is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line missing-auth".

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML used without sanitization — XSS vulnerability.
findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.message: Function "KeyboardNav" uses Math.random() — output is predictable.
findings.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.randomUUID() instead of Math.random().

findings.message: Server action "getDashboardStats" has no authentication check. Anyone can call it.
findings.fix: Add auth at the top of every exported server action (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) throw new Error('Unauthorized');") or wrap it with your auth guard. If it is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line nextjs-server-action-exposure".

findings.message: File uses "use server" directive but contains no authentication checks.
findings.fix: Add authentication checks to every exported function in this server action file.

findings.message: Database URL with credentials found in source code.
findings.fix: Move this Database URL with credentials to an environment variable. Add the variable to .env (git-ignored) and reference it via process.env.YOUR_KEY_NAME. Never commit secrets to source control.

findings.message: Non-localhost HTTP URL — data sent unencrypted
findings.fix: Use HTTPS for all external connections. HTTP transmits data in plaintext — anyone on the network can read it.

findings.message: Token/secret compared with === — vulnerable to timing attack
findings.fix: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual() instead of === for comparing tokens, secrets, and signatures. The === operator short-circuits on the first different character, leaking timing information. Example: crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(a), Buffer.from(b)).

findings.message: Sensitive data stored in browser storage — visible in DevTools → Application tab
findings.fix: Don't store tokens or sensitive data in localStorage/sessionStorage. Use httpOnly cookies (set by the server) for auth tokens — they're invisible to JavaScript and DevTools Application tab. For session data, keep it server-side.

findings.message: "randomCode" generated with Math.random() — predictable, not cryptographically secure.
findings.evidence: showNotification('Generate a weapon first before adding a style reference!');
findings.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes(32).toString("hex") or crypto.randomUUID() for security-sensitive values.

findings.message: "VITE_LOGIN_PASSWORD" exposes a secret to the browser. The VITE_ prefix makes this variable public in your build output.
findings.fix: Remove the "VITE_" prefix from this variable. Server-side secrets must NOT use client-prefixed env vars. Access it only in server-side code (API routes, server components, middleware).

findings.message: Password reset token generated with Math.random() — predictable.
findings.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes(32).toString("hex") for reset tokens. Never use Math.random() for security tokens.

findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Headers are configured but X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors is missing — vulnerable to clickjacking.
findings.fix: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY header, or use CSP frame-ancestors: 'none'. If using helmet: helmet() sets this by default.

findings.message: Database query without field selection — consider selecting only needed fields
findings.fix: Only return the fields the client actually needs. Use select/projection in your database query, or map the result to a DTO (data transfer object) before sending. Anyone can see the full response in DevTools → Network → click request → Response tab.

findings.message: Exported GET handler has no authentication check.
findings.fix: Add an auth check at the top of the handler (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) return Response.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 })") or wrap it with your auth guard. If this route is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line missing-auth".

If this route is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line missing-auth".

findings.message: Server action "QuickActions" has no authentication check. Anyone can call it.
findings.fix: Add auth at the top of every exported server action (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) throw new Error('Unauthorized');") or wrap it with your auth guard. If it is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line nextjs-server-action-exposure".

findings.message: Stripe webhook handler does not verify the signature — anyone can send fake events.
findings.fix: Verify the webhook: const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET). Never parse the body as JSON first — use the raw body.

findings.message: Middleware file contains no authentication or redirect logic.
findings.fix: Add auth checking in middleware: verify session/token and redirect unauthenticated users to login.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Non-localhost HTTP URL — data sent unencrypted
findings.evidence: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-li
findings.fix: Use HTTPS for all external connections. HTTP transmits data in plaintext — anyone on the network can read it.

findings.message: Next.js mutation handler — verify CSRF protection is in place
findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: Mongoose find() with no limit() — returns all matching documents
findings.fix: Always add pagination or limits to list endpoints. Use limit/offset, cursor-based pagination, or at minimum a hard cap (e.g., .limit(100)). Without limits: (1) anyone can scrape your entire database in one request, (2) large result sets crash your server or client, (3) it's a trivial DoS vector.

findings.message: Login endpoint has no brute force protection — unlimited password attempts allowed.
findings.fix: Add brute force protection to login endpoints: (1) Track failed attempts per user/IP. (2) Lock accounts after 5-10 failed attempts. (3) Add exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s delays). (4) Consider CAPTCHA after 3 failures. Global rate limiting alone isn't enough — an attacker can distribute attempts across IPs.

findings.message: Client-side auth redirect — only hides UI, does not protect data
findings.fix: Client-side auth only hides UI elements — it does NOT protect data. Always verify auth server-side in your API routes/middleware too. Anyone can open DevTools Console and call fetch("/your/api/endpoint") directly.

findings.message: Regex with nested quantifiers may cause catastrophic backtracking on malicious input.
findings.fix: Simplify the regex to avoid nested quantifiers. Use atomic groups or possessive quantifiers if supported. Add input length limits before regex matching. Consider using a regex linter like safe-regex.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Strict-Transport-Security
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Non-localhost HTTP URL — data sent unencrypted
findings.fix: Use HTTPS for all external connections. HTTP transmits data in plaintext — anyone on the network can read it.

findings.message: Database query without field selection — consider selecting only needed fields
findings.fix: Only return the fields the client actually needs. Use select/projection in your database query, or map the result to a DTO (data transfer object) before sending. Anyone can see the full response in DevTools → Network → click request → Response tab.

findings.message: Database query without field selection — consider selecting only needed fields

findings.message: Empty catch block — errors silently swallowed
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: Function "<anonymous>" handles file uploads without type/size validation in scope.
findings.fix: Validate MIME type against an allowlist and set a max file size. The HTML accept="" attribute is NOT security.

findings.message: CORS middleware with no configuration (defaults to allow all)
findings.fix: Configure CORS with specific allowed origins instead of "*". List only the domains that need access to your API.

findings.message: State-changing route with no CSRF protection
findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: API route calls a paid service but has no rate limiting. A bot or abusive user could run up your bill.
findings.fix: Add rate limiting before calling paid APIs. Use Upstash Ratelimit (serverless-friendly) or express-rate-limit. Set sensible per-user and global limits. Also consider adding spend alerts on your API provider dashboard.

findings.message: AI API call has no max_tokens limit — uncapped cost per request.
findings.fix: Add max_tokens (e.g., max_tokens: 1000) to limit cost per request. Also add rate limiting to the API route and set spend alerts on your AI provider dashboard.

findings.message: Client-side cookie set without security flags
findings.fix: Ensure cookies include httpOnly, secure, and sameSite flags. Check DevTools → Application → Cookies to verify flags are set correctly in production.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Hardcoded password detected.
findings.fix: Move credentials to environment variables. Use process.env or a secrets manager. Never store passwords or connection strings in source code.

findings.message: Direct innerHTML assignment with dynamic, unescaped value — potential XSS vector
findings.fix: Escape/sanitize the value before assigning to innerHTML (e.g. an esc()/DOMPurify wrapper), or use textContent for plain text.

findings.message: Direct innerHTML assignment with dynamic, unescaped value — potential XSS vector
findings.fix: Escape/sanitize the value before assigning to innerHTML (e.g. an esc()/DOMPurify wrapper), or use textContent for plain text.

findings.message: API key hardcoded in client-side code — visible in DevTools Sources
findings.fix: Never put secrets in client-side code. Move sensitive operations to a server-side API route. The browser bundle is fully readable — open DevTools → Sources and search for any string.

findings.message: Variable "API_KEY" contains a high-entropy string (entropy: 4.2) — possible hardcoded secret.
findings.fix: Move this value to an environment variable. If it's a secret, add it to .env (git-ignored) and reference via process.env.

findings.message: State-changing route with no CSRF protection
findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML used without sanitization — XSS vulnerability.
findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: Login endpoint has no brute force protection — unlimited password attempts allowed.
findings.fix: Add brute force protection to login endpoints: (1) Track failed attempts per user/IP. (2) Lock accounts after 5-10 failed attempts. (3) Add exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s delays). (4) Consider CAPTCHA after 3 failures. Global rate limiting alone isn't enough — an attacker can distribute attempts across IPs.

findings.message: Logging statement may contain personally identifiable information (PII).
findings.evidence: console.log(`Sending email alert to ${body.email} with ${body.opportunities.length} opportunities`);
findings.fix: Remove PII from log statements or redact sensitive fields before logging. Use structured logging with field allowlists.

findings.message: User input interpolated directly into LLM prompt — vulnerable to prompt injection.
findings.fix: Separate system prompts from user input. Use the "user" message role for user content, never embed user input in the "system" prompt. Add input validation and length limits.

findings.message: Exported GET handler has no authentication check.
findings.fix: Add an auth check at the top of the handler (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) return Response.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 })") or wrap it with your auth guard. If this route is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line missing-auth".

findings.message: Prisma query without select/include — returns ALL fields, potentially exposing sensitive data
findings.evidence: const setting = await prisma.setting.findUnique({ where: { key: 'anthropicApiKey' } })
findings.fix: Only return the fields the client actually needs. Use select/projection in your database query, or map the result to a DTO (data transfer object) before sending. Anyone can see the full response in DevTools → Network → click request → Response tab.

findings.message: Prisma findMany with no take/cursor — returns all matching records
findings.evidence: const all = await prisma.bookmark.findMany({ select: { id: true }, orderBy: { id: 'asc' } })
findings.fix: Always add pagination or limits to list endpoints. Use limit/offset, cursor-based pagination, or at minimum a hard cap (e.g., .limit(100)). Without limits: (1) anyone can scrape your entire database in one request, (2) large result sets crash your server or client, (3) it's a trivial DoS vector.

findings.message: Prisma query without select/include — returns ALL fields, potentially exposing sensitive data
findings.fix: Only return the fields the client actually needs. Use select/projection in your database query, or map the result to a DTO (data transfer object) before sending. Anyone can see the full response in DevTools → Network → click request → Response tab.

findings.message: Empty catch block — errors silently swallowed
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Sensitive data in console.log — visible in DevTools Console
findings.evidence: console.log('[x-sync] Skipping scheduled sync: missing credentials')
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Headers are configured but X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors is missing — vulnerable to clickjacking.
findings.fix: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY header, or use CSP frame-ancestors: 'none'. If using helmet: helmet() sets this by default.

findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Database query without field selection — consider selecting only needed fields
findings.fix: Only return the fields the client actually needs. Use select/projection in your database query, or map the result to a DTO (data transfer object) before sending. Anyone can see the full response in DevTools → Network → click request → Response tab.

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: Firebase Admin SDK used in client-side code — grants full database/auth access.
findings.fix: Move Firebase Admin SDK usage to server-side API routes or Cloud Functions. Use the regular Firebase client SDK for client-side code.

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML used without sanitization — XSS vulnerability.
findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.message: Client-side cookie set without security flags
findings.fix: Ensure cookies include httpOnly, secure, and sameSite flags. Check DevTools → Application → Cookies to verify flags are set correctly in production.

findings.message: MFA verification endpoint may not be linked to the login session — could be called independently.

findings.message: AI response set as innerHTML — AI output can contain malicious content.

findings.message: Empty catch block — errors silently swallowed
findings.evidence: try { console.log('Media files saved:', fs.readdirSync(dir + '/tweets_media').length); } catch (e) { }
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Logging statement may contain personally identifiable information (PII).
findings.fix: Remove PII from log statements or redact sensitive fields before logging. Use structured logging with field allowlists.

findings.message: Variable "key" contains a high-entropy string (entropy: 4.1) — possible hardcoded secret.
findings.fix: Move this value to an environment variable. If it's a secret, add it to .env (git-ignored) and reference via process.env.

findings.message: Direct __proto__ access — potential prototype pollution vector
findings.fix: Don't deep-merge raw user input into objects. Either: (1) Destructure only expected fields. (2) Use a safe merge library that blocks __proto__ and constructor.prototype. (3) Freeze Object.prototype. (4) Use schema validation (Zod/Yup) to strip unexpected keys. An attacker sending {"__proto__":{"isAdmin":true}} can modify every object in your app.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: vercel.json has no security headers configured.
findings.fix: Add a "headers" section with X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Content-Security-Policy.

findings.message: User data logged to console — visible in DevTools
findings.evidence: try { console.log('Profile media:', fs.readdirSync(dir + '/profile_media').length); } catch(e) { console.log('Profile me
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.fix: Move the IP address to an environment variable: process.env.SERVICE_HOST. Hardcoded IPs make deployment inflexible and may expose infrastructure details.

findings.message: Sensitive data stored in browser storage — visible in DevTools → Application tab
findings.fix: Don't store tokens or sensitive data in localStorage/sessionStorage. Use httpOnly cookies (set by the server) for auth tokens — they're invisible to JavaScript and DevTools Application tab. For session data, keep it server-side.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Shell command with user input — command injection risk
findings.fix: Sanitize all user input before use. For HTML: use a sanitization library or textContent instead of innerHTML. For SQL: use parameterized queries. For shell: use allowlists, never interpolate user input into commands.

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML used without sanitization — XSS vulnerability.
findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.message: Server action "getAgentAction" has no authentication check. Anyone can call it.
findings.fix: Add auth at the top of every exported server action (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) throw new Error('Unauthorized');") or wrap it with your auth guard. If it is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line nextjs-server-action-exposure".

findings.message: Server action "getAvailableAgentActions" has no authentication check. Anyone can call it.
findings.fix: Add auth at the top of every exported server action (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) throw new Error('Unauthorized');") or wrap it with your auth guard. If it is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line nextjs-server-action-exposure".

findings.fix: Add auth at the top of every exported server action (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) throw new Error('Unauthorized');") or wrap it with your auth guard. If it is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line nextjs-server-action-exposure".

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy

findings.message: Token/secret compared with === — vulnerable to timing attack
findings.fix: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual() instead of === for comparing tokens, secrets, and signatures. The === operator short-circuits on the first different character, leaking timing information. Example: crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(a), Buffer.from(b)).

findings.message: Token/secret compared with === — vulnerable to timing attack
findings.fix: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual() instead of === for comparing tokens, secrets, and signatures. The === operator short-circuits on the first different character, leaking timing information. Example: crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(a), Buffer.from(b)).

findings.message: Non-localhost HTTP URL — data sent unencrypted
findings.fix: Use HTTPS for all external connections. HTTP transmits data in plaintext — anyone on the network can read it.

findings.message: Sensitive data in console.log — visible in DevTools Console
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.message: Sensitive data in console.log — visible in DevTools Console
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.message: Sensitive data in console.log — visible in DevTools Console
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.message: Logging statement may contain personally identifiable information (PII).
findings.fix: Remove PII from log statements or redact sensitive fields before logging. Use structured logging with field allowlists.

findings.message: Stack trace potentially exposed
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Logging statement may contain personally identifiable information (PII).
findings.fix: Remove PII from log statements or redact sensitive fields before logging. Use structured logging with field allowlists.

findings.message: User data logged to console — visible in DevTools
findings.evidence: console.log("[Dev] Using separate userData path:", devUserData)
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.message: User data logged to console — visible in DevTools
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.message: User data logged to console — visible in DevTools
findings.fix: Remove console.log statements that output sensitive data before deploying. Use a proper logging library with log levels and strip debug/info logs in production builds. Anyone can open DevTools → Console and see everything you logged.

findings.message: Error logged with console.log instead of console.error
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Error logged with console.log instead of console.error
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML used without sanitization — XSS vulnerability.
findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.message: Client-side cookie set without security flags
findings.fix: Ensure cookies include httpOnly, secure, and sameSite flags. Check DevTools → Application → Cookies to verify flags are set correctly in production.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: Possible Heroku API key / UUID secret found in source code.
findings.fix: Move this Possible Heroku API key / UUID secret to an environment variable. Add the variable to .env (git-ignored) and reference it via process.env.YOUR_KEY_NAME. Never commit secrets to source control.

findings.message: Secret passed as URL query parameter — logged in server logs, browser history, and Referer headers
findings.fix: Never pass secrets in URL query parameters. Use Authorization headers instead: "headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + token }". URLs are logged in server access logs, proxy logs, CDN logs, browser history, and sent in the Referer header to every external site you link to.

findings.message: Direct innerHTML assignment with dynamic, unescaped value — potential XSS vector
findings.fix: Escape/sanitize the value before assigning to innerHTML (e.g. an esc()/DOMPurify wrapper), or use textContent for plain text.

findings.evidence: console.log(`[Auth] OTP sent to admin for user: ${email}`);
findings.fix: Remove PII from log statements or redact sensitive fields before logging. Use structured logging with field allowlists.

findings.message: State-changing route with no CSRF protection
findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: MFA verification endpoint may not be linked to the login session — could be called independently.
findings.fix: Link MFA verification to the login session. Issue a temporary "pending MFA" token after password auth, require it for the MFA verify endpoint, and only issue a full session after both steps pass.

findings.message: Exported GET handler has no authentication check.
findings.fix: Add an auth check at the top of the handler (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) return Response.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 })") or wrap it with your auth guard. If this route is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line missing-auth".

findings.fix: Sanitize all user input before use. For HTML: use a sanitization library or textContent instead of innerHTML. For SQL: use parameterized queries. For shell: use allowlists, never interpolate user input into commands.

findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: Stack trace potentially exposed
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.

findings.message: Next.js mutation handler — verify CSRF protection is in place
findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: API route calls a paid service but has no rate limiting. A bot or abusive user could run up your bill.
findings.fix: Add rate limiting before calling paid APIs. Use Upstash Ratelimit (serverless-friendly) or express-rate-limit. Set sensible per-user and global limits. Also consider adding spend alerts on your API provider dashboard.

findings.message: Exported GET handler has no authentication check.
findings.fix: Add an auth check at the top of the handler (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) return Response.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 })") or wrap it with your auth guard. If this route is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line missing-auth".

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML used without sanitization — XSS vulnerability.
findings.fix: Sanitize the HTML before rendering (e.g. DOMPurify.sanitize(content)) and pass the result to __html. If you use a custom escaper, add its name to .vibe-audit.json "customEscapers". Better yet, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML and rely on React's built-in escaping.

findings.message: Non-localhost HTTP URL — data sent unencrypted
findings.fix: Use HTTPS for all external connections. HTTP transmits data in plaintext — anyone on the network can read it.

findings.fix: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY header, or use CSP frame-ancestors: 'none'. If using helmet: helmet() sets this by default.

findings.message: OAuth flow has no state parameter — vulnerable to CSRF (login CSRF / account takeover).
findings.evidence: * Uses Reddit's OAuth2 API for fetching posts from AI-related subreddits
findings.fix: Generate a random state parameter, store in session, add to OAuth URL, and verify it in the callback: const state = crypto.randomUUID(); session.oauthState = state; // add &state=... to URL.

findings.message: Direct Supabase query builder called client-side (e.g. supabase.from().select())
findings.fix: Move database query logic to the server (Server Actions, API routes, or server-only modules) and call that endpoint from the client. Direct client-side queries expose your schema and bypass server-side security controls.

findings.message: Check-then-update on balance/inventory without lock — concurrent requests can cause inconsistency.
findings.fix: Use database transactions or atomic operations. For Prisma: prisma.$transaction(). For MongoDB: $inc for counters. For SQL: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE within a transaction.

findings.message: Function "POST" handles file uploads without type/size validation in scope.
findings.fix: Validate MIME type against an allowlist and set a max file size. The HTML accept="" attribute is NOT security.

findings.message: Function "POST" passes raw request body to a database operation without destructuring or schema validation first.
findings.fix: Destructure only expected fields: "const { name, email } = req.body" then pass those explicitly. Or validate with Zod: "schema.parse(req.body)".

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: Middleware file contains no authentication or redirect logic.
findings.fix: Add auth checking in middleware: verify session/token and redirect unauthenticated users to login.

findings.message: Query builder select without limit — may return unbounded results
findings.fix: Always add pagination or limits to list endpoints. Use limit/offset, cursor-based pagination, or at minimum a hard cap (e.g., .limit(100)). Without limits: (1) anyone can scrape your entire database in one request, (2) large result sets crash your server or client, (3) it's a trivial DoS vector.

Without limits: (1) anyone can scrape your entire database in one request, (2) large result sets crash your server or client, (3) it's a trivial DoS vector.

findings.fix: Escape/sanitize the value before assigning to innerHTML (e.g. an esc()/DOMPurify wrapper), or use textContent for plain text.

findings.message: "randomKey" generated with Math.random() — predictable, not cryptographically secure.
findings.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes(32).toString("hex") or crypto.randomUUID() for security-sensitive values.

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: Logout handler does not destroy the server-side session — old session tokens remain valid.
findings.fix: Add session.destroy() or req.logout() in the logout handler. Clearing cookies alone does not invalidate the session server-side. For JWT auth, add the token to a server-side revocation list on logout.

findings.message: Exported GET handler has no authentication check.
findings.fix: Add an auth check at the top of the handler (e.g. "const session = await getServerSession(); if (!session) return Response.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 })") or wrap it with your auth guard. If this route is intentionally public, add "// vibe-audit-ignore-next-line missing-auth".

findings.message: Middleware file contains no authentication or redirect logic.
findings.fix: Add auth checking in middleware: verify session/token and redirect unauthenticated users to login.

findings.message: Hardcoded password detected.
findings.fix: Move credentials to environment variables. Use process.env or a secrets manager. Never store passwords or connection strings in source code.

findings.message: Hardcoded password detected.

findings.message: Database URL with credentials found in source code.
findings.fix: Move this Database URL with credentials to an environment variable. Add the variable to .env (git-ignored) and reference it via process.env.YOUR_KEY_NAME. Never commit secrets to source control.

findings.message: Database connection string with credentials detected.
findings.fix: Move credentials to environment variables. Use process.env or a secrets manager. Never store passwords or connection strings in source code.

findings.message: Stripe webhook handler does not verify the signature — anyone can send fake events.
findings.fix: Verify the webhook: const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET). Never parse the body as JSON first — use the raw body.

findings.message: Stripe webhook handler does not verify the signature — anyone can send fake events.
findings.fix: Verify the webhook: const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET). Never parse the body as JSON first — use the raw body.

findings.message: Sensitive data in hidden form field — trivially editable via DevTools Elements tab
findings.fix: Never trust client-side values for pricing, permissions, or business logic. Always recalculate/revalidate on the server. The client is the user's machine — they control everything on it. DevTools lets anyone modify any value, re-enable any button, and edit any request before it's sent.

findings.message: API route calls a paid service but has no rate limiting. A bot or abusive user could run up your bill.
findings.fix: Add rate limiting before calling paid APIs. Use Upstash Ratelimit (serverless-friendly) or express-rate-limit. Set sensible per-user and global limits. Also consider adding spend alerts on your API provider dashboard.

findings.message: Next.js mutation handler — verify CSRF protection is in place
findings.fix: Add CSRF protection to state-changing endpoints. Options: (1) Use SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies. (2) Implement double-submit cookie pattern. (3) Check Origin/Referer headers. (4) Use a CSRF token library like csrf-csrf. Without CSRF protection, attackers can trigger actions on behalf of your users from external sites.

findings.message: Config file is missing security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.evidence: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy
findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.fix: Add security headers to your deployment config or middleware. At minimum set: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

findings.message: User object serialized to browser storage — may contain sensitive fields visible in DevTools
findings.fix: Don't store tokens or sensitive data in localStorage/sessionStorage. Use httpOnly cookies (set by the server) for auth tokens — they're invisible to JavaScript and DevTools Application tab. For session data, keep it server-side.

findings.evidence: console.warn("Missing NEXT_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable. AI features will not work.");

findings.message: Auto-incrementing ID — sequential and enumerable
findings.fix: Use UUIDs (crypto.randomUUID()) or CUIDs instead of auto-incrementing integers for public-facing IDs. Sequential IDs let attackers enumerate every record by incrementing the number. Keep autoincrement for internal DB use but expose a random public ID.
